Temperature Core.name: TEMPERATURE_RESULT
Temperature Core.description: 16-bit, read-only register that stores the output of the most recent conversion. One LSB equals 7.8125 m°C. Data are represented in binary two's complement format.

fields.name: T
fields.description: 16-bit, read-only register that stores the most recent temperature conversion results.

fields.name: HIGH_ALERT
fields.description: High Alert flag: 1: Set when the conversion result is higher than the high limit 0: Cleared on read of configuration register Therm mode: 1: Set when the conversion result is higher than the therm limit 0: Cleared when the conversion result is lower than the hysteresis

fields.name: LOW_ALERT
fields.description: Low Alert flag: 1: Set when the conversion result is lower than the low limit 0: Cleared when the configuration register is read Therm mode: Always set to 0

fields.name: DATA_READY
fields.description: Data ready flag. This flag indicates that the conversion is complete and the temperature register can be read. Every time the temperature register or configuration register is read, this bit is cleared. This bit is set at the end of the conversion when the temperature register is updated. Data ready can be monitored on the ALERT pin by setting bit 2 of the configuration register.

fields.name: EEPROM_BUSY
fields.description: EEPROM busy flag. The value of the flag indicates that the EEPROM is busy during programming or power-up.

fields.name: MOD
fields.description: Set conversion mode. 00: Continuous conversion (CC) 01: Shutdown (SD) 10: Continuous conversion (CC), Same as 00 (reads back = 00) 11: One-shot conversion (OS)

fields.name: CONV
fields.description: Conversion cycle bit. See Table 7-7 for the standby time between conversions.

fields.name: AVG
fields.description: Conversion averaging modes. Determines the number of conversion results that are collected and averaged before updating the temperature register. The average is an accumulated average and not a running average. 00: No averaging 01: 8 Averaged conversions 10: 32 averaged conversions 11: 64 averaged conversions

fields.name: T_N_A
fields.description: Therm/alert mode select. 1: Therm mode 0: Alert mode

fields.name: POL
fields.description: ALERT pin polarity bit. 1: Active high 0: Active low

fields.name: DR_ALERT
fields.description: ALERT pin select bit. 1: ALERT pin reflects the status of the data ready flag 0: ALERT pin reflects the status of the alert flags

enum_values.0: ALERT pin reflects the status of the alert flags
enum_values.1: ALERT pin reflects the status of the data ready flag

Temperature Core.name: HIGH_LIMIT
Temperature Core.description: 16-bit, read/write register that stores the high limit for comparison with the temperature result. One LSB equals 7.8125 m°C. The range of the register is ±256 °C. Negative numbers are represented in binary two's complement format. Following power-up or a general-call reset, the high-limit register is loaded with the stored value from the EEPROM.

fields.name: H
fields.description: 16-bit, read/write register that stores the high limit for comparison with the temperature result.

Temperature Core.name: LOW_LIMIT
Temperature Core.description: 16-bit, read/write register that stores the low limit for comparison with the temperature result. One LSB equals 7.8125 m°C. The range of the register is ±256 °C. Negative numbers are represented in binary two's complement format. Following power-up or reset, the low-limit register is loaded with the stored value from the EEPROM.

fields.name: L
fields.description: 16-bit, read/write register that stores the low limit for comparison with the temperature result.

Temperature Core.name: TEMPERATURE_OFFSET
Temperature Core.description: User-defined temperature offset register during system calibration.

fields.name: D
fields.description: Temperature offset data from system calibration.

fields.name: EUN
fields.description: EEPROM unlock. 0: EEPROM is locked for programming: writes to all EEPROM addresses (such as configuration, limits, and EEPROM locations 1-4) are written to registers in digital logic and are not programmed in the EEPROM 1: EEPROM unlocked for programming: any writes to programmable registers program the respective location in the EEPROM

fields.name: EEPROM_BUSY
fields.description: EEPROM busy. This flag is the mirror of the EEPROM busy flag (bit 12) in the configuration register. 0: Indicates that the EEPROM is ready, which means that the EEPROM has finished the last transaction and is ready to accept new commands 1: Indicates that the EEPROM is busy, which means that the EEPROM is currently completing a programming operation or performing power-up on reset load

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Not used

EEPROM.name: EEPROM1
EEPROM.description: 16-bit register that be used as a scratch pad by the customer to store generalpurpose data. This register has a corresponding EEPROM location. Writes to this address when the EEPROM is locked write data into the register and not to the EEPROM. Writes to this register when the EEPROM is unlocked causes the corresponding EEPROM location to be programmed. EEPROM[4:1] are preprogrammed during manufacturing with the unique ID that can be overwritten. To support NIST traceability do not delete or reprogram the EEPROM[1] register.

fields.name: D
fields.description: This 16-bit register can be used as a scratch pad. To support NIST traceability do not delete or re-program this register.

fields.name: D
fields.description: This 16-bit register can be used as a scratch pad.

EEPROM.name: EEPROM3
EEPROM.description: 16-bit scratch pad register. Do not delete or reprogram.

fields.name: D
fields.description: This 16-bit register is used as a scratch pad. To support NIST traceability, do not delete or re-program this register.

System.name: DEVICE_ID
System.description: Read-only register indicating the device ID.

fields.name: REV
fields.description: Indicates the revision number.

fields.name: DID
fields.description: Indicates the device ID.
